title: Collision-Spread Hash Offsets — a large progression-free set in Z_p
strategies: no-carry base-3 {0,1}-digit set restricted to [0, p/2) (classic Salem-Spencer construction; progression-free mod p because no modular wraparound occurs)
greedy Stanley-sequence scan in Z_p: add offsets in increasing order, skipping any that complete a 3-AP with the current set
Behrend sphere construction: digits on a fixed-radius sphere in a high base, mapped to a wide register
affine/multiplier transforms of a known progression-free set followed by greedy augmentation over the unused residues
restricting an integer progression-free set to a sub-interval so modular wraparound cannot create a progression
